en.extract:
ʻŌmaʻo (literally, "green" in Hawaiian) is a census-designated place (CDP) in Kauaʻi County, Hawaiʻi, United States. The population was 1,346 at the 2020 census.


Geography
ʻŌmaʻo is located on the south side of the island of Kauaʻi at 21°55′11″N 159°28′54″W (21.919852, -159.481689). It is bordered to the west by Lawai and to the southeast by Koloa. Hawaii Route 50 passes through the north side of the community, leading northeast 9 miles (14 km) to Lihue and west 8 miles (13 km) to Hanapepe.
According to the United States Census Bureau, the Omao CDP has a total area of 1.3 square miles (3.3 km2), of which 0.02 square miles (0.05 km2), or 1.43%, are water.


Demographics

As of the census of 2000, there were 1,221 people, 392 households, and 297 families residing in the CDP.  The population density was 1,018.9 inhabitants per square mile (393.4/km2).  There were 422 housing units at an average density of 352.1 per square mile (135.9/km2).  The racial makeup of the CDP was 39.6% White, 0.2% African American, 0.9% Native American, 28.5% Asian, 5.7% Pacific Islander, 1.3% from other races, and 23.8% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 12.9% of the population.
There were 392 households, out of which 38.3% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 59.7% were married couples living together, 10.7% had a female householder with no husband present, and 24.2% were non-families. 17.3% of all households were made up of individuals, and 7.1% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older.  The average household size was 2.97 and the average family size was 3.36.
In the CDP the population was spread out, with 26.2% under the age of 18, 6.2% from 18 to 24, 29.1% from 25 to 44, 23.6% from 45 to 64, and 14.9% who were 65 years of age or older.  The median age was 39 years. For every 100 females, there were 104.9 males.  For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 99.3 males.
The median income for a household in the CDP was $53,750, and the median income for a family was $61,042. Males had a median income of $30,300 versus $27,647 for females. The per capita income for the CDP was $20,175.  About 6.0% of families and 10.0% of the population were below the poverty line, including 9.0% of those under age 18 and 20.0% of those age 65 or over.


References

'''{{okina}}Ōma{{okina}}o''' (literally, "green" in [[Hawaiian Language|Hawaiian]]<ref>{{Cite book | author=Pukui, Mary Kawena | title=Place Names of Hawaii | year=1974 | url=https://archive.org/details/placenamesofhawa0000puku | url-access=registration | publisher=University of Hawaii Press | isbn=0-8248-0524-0 }}</ref>) is a [[census-designated place]] (CDP) in [[Kauaʻi County, Hawaii|Kaua{{okina}}i County]], [[Hawaii|Hawai{{okina}}i]], United States. The population was 1,346 at the [[2020 United States census|2020 census]].

==Geography==
{{okina}}Ōma{{okina}}o is located on the south side of the island of [[Kauaʻi]] at {{coord|21|55|11|N|159|28|54|W|type:city}} (21.919852, -159.481689).<ref name="GR1">{{cite web|url=https://www.census.gov/geographies/reference-files/time-series/geo/gazetteer-files.html|publisher=[[United States Census Bureau]]|access-date=2011-04-23|date=2011-02-12|title=US Gazetteer files: 2010, 2000, and 1990}}</ref> It is bordered to the west by [[Lawai, Hawaii|Lawai]] and to the southeast by [[Koloa, Hawaii|Koloa]]. [[Hawaii Route 50]] passes through the north side of the community, leading northeast {{convert|9|mi|0}} to [[Lihue, Hawaii|Lihue]] and west {{convert|8|mi|0}} to [[Hanapepe, Hawaii|Hanapepe]].

According to the [[United States Census Bureau]], the Omao CDP has a total area of {{convert|3.3|km2|order=flip}}, of which {{convert|0.05|km2|order=flip|2}}, or 1.43%, are water.<ref name="Census 2010">{{Cite web| url=http://factfinder2.census.gov/bkmk/table/1.0/en/DEC/10_DP/G001/1600000US1557800| title=Geographic Identifiers: 2010 Demographic Profile Data (G001): Omao CDP, Hawaii| publisher=U.S. Census Bureau| work=American Factfinder| access-date=February 5, 2019}}{{dead link|bot=medic|date=April 2020}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

==Demographics==
{{US Census population
|1990= 1142
|2000= 1221
|2010= 1301
|2020= 1346
|footnote=source:<ref>{{cite web
  |url=https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/decennial-census.html
  |title=CENSUS OF POPULATION AND HOUSING (1790-2000)
  |publisher=[[U.S. Census Bureau]]
  |access-date=2010-07-31}}</ref>
}}
As of the [[census]]<ref name="GR2">{{cite web|url=https://www.census.gov|publisher=[[United States Census Bureau]]|access-date=2008-01-31|title=U.S. Census website}}</ref> of 2000, there were 1,221 people, 392 households, and 297 families residing in the CDP.  The population density was {{convert|1,018.9|PD/sqmi|PD/km2|sp=us|adj=off}}.  There were 422 housing units at an average density of {{convert|352.1|/sqmi|/km2|sp=us|adj=off}}.  The racial makeup of the CDP was 39.6% [[White (U.S. Census)|White]], 0.2% [[African American (U.S. Census)|African American]], 0.9% [[Native American (U.S. Census)|Native American]], 28.5% [[Asian (U.S. Census)|Asian]], 5.7% [[Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)|Pacific Islander]], 1.3% from [[Race (United States Census)|other races]], and 23.8% from two or more races. [[Hispanic (U.S. Census)|Hispanic]] or [[Latino (U.S. Census)|Latino]] of any race were 12.9% of the population.

There were 392 households, out of which 38.3% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 59.7% were [[Marriage|married couples]] living together, 10.7% had a female householder with no husband present, and 24.2% were non-families. 17.3% of all households were made up of individuals, and 7.1% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older.  The average household size was 2.97 and the average family size was 3.36.

In the CDP the population was spread out, with 26.2% under the age of 18, 6.2% from 18 to 24, 29.1% from 25 to 44, 23.6% from 45 to 64, and 14.9% who were 65 years of age or older.  The median age was 39 years. For every 100 females, there were 104.9 males.  For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 99.3 males.

The median income for a household in the CDP was $53,750, and the median income for a family was $61,042. Males had a median income of $30,300 versus $27,647 for females. The [[per capita income]] for the CDP was $20,175.  About 6.0% of families and 10.0% of the population were below the [[poverty line]], including 9.0% of those under age 18 and 20.0% of those age 65 or over.

zh.extract:
奧馬奧（夏威夷語：ʻŌmaʻo，意指「綠色」）是位於美國夏威夷州可愛縣的一個人口普查指定地區。


地理
奧馬奧的座標為21°55′11″N 159°28′54″W，而該地最高點為海拔高度179米（即587英尺）。


人口

根據2010年美國人口普查的數據，奧馬奧的面積為3.32平方千米，當中陸地面積為3.28平方千米，而水域面積為0.04平方千米。當地共有人口1301人，而人口密度為每平方千米391.87人。
